entries.title: Newsletter: Zelenskyy in town for talks with NATO and EU leaders
entries.description: Also in this newsletter: Former US commander general Ben Hodges reacts to the signing of the US-Iran peace deal, and former French Economy Minister Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews the EU needs to function as a core group of six.

entries.title: Watch: Behind closed doors—Ukraine, taxes, and trade at this week's European Council Summit
entries.description: Tonight, Council President António Costa is gathering all twenty-seven national leaders for an exclusive, high-stakes working dinner. Sadly, he forgot to invite your reporter, but that doesn't stop us from seeing what will be served on the multi-course menu.

entries.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
entries.description: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

entries.title: Crimea will turn into an island, Ukraine’s defence minister says in a message to Russia
entries.description: Ukraine is methodically cutting off ground lines of communication for Russian forces into occupied Crimea, isolating the peninsula from Moscow’s forces and any supplies from Russia.

entries.title: Discover Lisbon like a local with our guide: Seven insider tips for its seven hills
entries.description: Venture off the beaten track and out of your comfort zone, dodge the crowds and, above all, steer clear of tourist traps. Here are seven ideas, one for each of Lisbon’s hills, devised by locals.

entries.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
entries.description: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

entries.title: Waldorf Astoria to open in London's iconic Admiralty Arch in autumn 2026 - in pictures
entries.description: Located on The Mall, the restored Grade I-listed landmark has been transformed into a luxury hotel with 114 rooms and suites, a spa, and two signature restaurants.

entries.title: 'The Ring' and 'Lilo & Stitch' actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
entries.description: Horror movie fans will recognize Chase from her performance as Samara Morgan, the main antagonist in American remake of 'The Ring'.

entries.title: Hegseth says Europe must take the lead on building 'NATO 3.0'
entries.description: US president Donald Trump has repeatedly pushed for European NATO allies to divert more money to defence spending.

entries.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
entries.description: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

entries.title: VivaTech 2026: Tips from L'Oréal and PwC on personal vitality and business resilience
entries.description: From skincare giant L'Oréal to consulting firm PwC, business leaders at VivaTech 2026 had a shared message: personal vitality and organisational resilience are no longer a luxury — they are a competitive necessity.

entries.title: Where is EV charging cheapest and fastest?
entries.description: Europe's EV charging network is growing fast, but prices and refuelling point density vary widely across the continent. Europe in Motion breaks down the data.

entries.title: Shipping industry wary of resuming Strait of Hormuz navigation despite imminent deal
entries.description: The world's most important energy chokepoint may soon reopen, but insurance markets must regain confidence for the shipping sector to resume normal navigation. Meanwhile, Germany has dispatched two naval vessels for a possible mine clearance operation.

entries.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
entries.description: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

entries.title: Cristiano Ronaldo faces media backlash as pundits slam Portugal's display against DR Congo
entries.description: The 41-year-old Portugal captain was hit by a barrage of criticism after a display that fell short of expectations at what is his sixth and, possibly, last World Cup.

entries.title: Misinformation targets Trump and European leaders at G7
entries.description: Social media users have been sharing old clips and taking statements by politicians out of context, in an apparent effort to highlight the strained relationship between the US and Europe.

entries.title: Voters head to the polls in key UK by-election that could determine Britain's next PM
entries.description: Burnham is regarded by many Labour MPs as the party's best chance of recovery following the disastrous May local elections.

entries.title: Inside the ultra-luxe Swiss mountainside retreat where Iran and the US are set to meet
entries.description: Initial negotiations between the US and Iran on the final agreement are set to take place at the Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne this Friday.

entries.title: Besides Trump, nobody believes deal will last, former US general says
entries.description: Retired US General Ben Hodges believes the situation is now "much worse" than four months ago. With Israel and Lebanon left out of the deal between the US and Iran, Hodges thinks the potential for Israel and Hezbollah to "resume fighting is really high".

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
entries.description: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

entries.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, so will Moscow,’ Zelenskyy says after refinery fire
entries.description: The Ukrainian president said on Thursday that Kyiv will respond to all Russian attacks and that the morning strike on Moscow’s refinery — in response to Russia's attack on a UNESCO-protected cathedral earlier this week was “entirely justified.”

entries.title: World's longest non-stop flight set for 2027 take-off as Qantas confirms Sydney-London route
entries.description: Qantas’s long-awaited ‘Project Sunrise’ will see the world's longest non-stop passenger flight cover more than 16,000 kilometres between 19 and 22 hours.

entries.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
entries.description: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

entries.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
entries.description: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

entries.title: England’s captain fantastic: Harry Kane praised for a ‘complete performance’ against Croatia
entries.description: The Bayern Munich striker scored twice as the Three Lions beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup group L opening fixture.

entries.title: Joel and Ethan Coen named as this year’s Lumière Award recipients
entries.description: The 18th Lumière Awards will honour not one but two giants of the silver screen...

entries.title: EU-backed green bonds risk financing Chinese clean tech in third countries
entries.description: The EU has launched a €15-20bn green investment fund to boost sustainable projects, but a good share of the money is set to go towards buying Chinese clean technologies – including the same high-risk power inverters Brussels is trying to phase out.

entries.title: From Foxconn to Nvidia: Why France is so attractive for Europe’s AI infrastructure
entries.description: Foxconn, Nvidia and Mistral AI announce major AI infrastructure deals at Europe's VivaTech conference, with France's cheap nuclear energy and homegrown talent drawing global investment.

entries.title: Salesforce France CEO: Both leaders and employees need to adapt to AI
entries.description: Speaking at Vivatech in Paris, Emilie Sidiqian, Salesforce France’s CEO, told Euronews Next how companies should embrace AI and why leaders must drive its adoption from the top.

entries.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old 'proto-Stonehenge' just a few miles from the famous monument
entries.description: The structure consisted of two wooden posts that aligned precisely with the summer sunrise and winter sunset, suggesting ancient Britons were already tracking the Sun before the creation of Stonehenge.

entries.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
entries.description: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

entries.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
entries.description: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

entries.title: Poland arrests suspect in killing of Russian artist critical of Putin, PM Donald Tusk says
entries.description: Since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has been accused of trying to assassinate its opponents abroad, including targeting exiled activists in France and Lithuania.

entries.title: Mundial 2026. Karykatura samego siebie - prasa światowa krytykuje Cristiano Ronaldo
entries.description: Międzynarodowa prasa ostro skrytykowała kapitana reprezentacji Portugalii po jego występie przeciwko Demokratycznej Republice Konga.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Toy Story 5' - You've got a friend in tech?
entries.description: The toys face a new existential crisis: technology. As timely as it is, can the fifth instalment recapture the magic lacking in 'Toy Story 4'?

entries.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama in Berlin: Europe today needs courage for enlargement
entries.description: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment? Albania's PM Edi Rama makes his case in Berlin

entries.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
entries.description: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

entries.title: Graft probe into Spanish ex-PM Zapatero expanded to daughters and secretary, court says
entries.description: The former premier defended his innocence during a three-hour hearing before Calama on Wednesday, becoming the first former or serving Spanish head of government to declare as a suspect in a corruption investigation.

entries.title: Scotland for the win: Massachusetts becomes first US state to 'legalise' haggis
entries.description: The Scottish staple has been illegal in the US since the 1970s. Following a pre-World Cup campaign by one of Scotland's leading butchers and the efforts of a Scottish podcaster, Massachusetts has become the first US state to finally legalise haggis. Officially. Ish.

entries.title: Rutte tries to downplay Pete Hegseth's blistering attack on NATO
entries.description: As the Trump admininstration plans to pull a number troops out of Europe in the next six months, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth lambasted NATO defence ministers in Brussels, describing them as "shameful" and preoccupied with gender equality and climate change.

entries.title: Middle East flights update: Which airlines are returning to the region and when?
entries.description: While the US and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding, European airlines are unlikely to restart flights until after the summer.

entries.title: Bulgarian prime minister opposes EU sanctions on Russia's Patriarch Kirill
entries.description: "The era of the Crusades is over," the Bulgarian prime minister said on Thursday as he confirmed his opposition to the proposal.

entries.title: Palestine Football Association denies reported FIFA youth match against Israel
entries.description: The PFA said it had "not received any official communication or proposal from FIFA regarding such a match".

entries.title: Chinese-British nationals jailed after being convicted of spying on exiled Hong Kong dissidents
entries.description: The sweeping National Security Law, which severely curtailed freedoms in Hong Kong, contributed to years of strained ties between Beijing and London.

entries.title: EU summit: Leaders and Zelenskyy to debate accession and peace negotiations
entries.description: Russia's war on Ukraine and unfair Chinese competition are expected to dominate the first day of the EU summit in Brussels.

entries.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
entries.description: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

entries.title: New Lithuanian coalition formed as Social Democrats strike deal with partners
entries.description: Lithuania’s Social Democrats have formed a third coalition in just one and a half years, clearing the way for a new cabinet to be led by party leader Mindaugas Sinkevičius.

entries.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
entries.description: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

entries.title: The energy transition's next test is security
entries.description: 'Countries that emerge strongest from the next phase of the transition will be those with diversified energy systems that remain secure, affordable and sustainable in a more uncertain world' writes Roberto Bocca from World Economic Forum’s Centre for Energy and Materials in an OpEd for Euronews.

entries.title: Keeping up appearances: Greece reveals Parthenon façade after 220 years
entries.description: For the first time in about 220 years, the west façade of the Parthenon in Greece can be see in its fullest possible form. External scaffolding has now been permanently removed from the monument, marking a key step in its long restoration.

entries.title: Bruce Springsteen, Stevie Wonder and Bono to attend Obama Centre's star-studded opening
entries.description: The $850 million campus, located on Chicago's South Side, includes a museum, library, sports facilities and community spaces.

entries.title: Switzerland heading towards referendum on construction of new nuclear plants
entries.description: During the debates in parliament, Energy Minister Albert Rosti stressed the need to keep the nuclear power option open to guarantee the country's long-term energy supply.

entries.title: Fact check: Can EU countries restrict or ban Israeli settlement imports?
entries.description: Several EU states are weighing measures against goods from Israeli settlements in the West Bank, East Jerusalem and the Golan Heights – all considered illegal under international law. Some argue that rules that exclude such goods from Israel’s tariff-free trade deal are not being properly enforced.

entries.title: French fishermen gather in Cherbourg as sector faces down multiple challenges
entries.description: In total, the French government said €13 million would be dedicated to the fishing sector for fuel consumption in April and May.

entries.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
entries.description: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
entries.description: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

entries.title: Companies are rushing into AI, but adoption is still lagging, a KPMG executive says
entries.description: Companies may have AI strategies, but few can prove they are paying off, according to KPMG’s recent report. Euronews Next spoke with Mathieu Wallich-Petit, Head of Clients & Markets at KPMG France about how businesses can move from AI pilots to real impact.

entries.title: Foreign aid cuts and climate change pushing up migrant flows, IOM chief warns
entries.description: Several rich Western countries, particularly the United States but also many European nations, have cut their development aid budgets in recent years.

entries.title: UK police say man under arrest after toddler 'ended up' in crocodile pen at zoo
entries.description: Cambridgeshire Constabulary said the toddler had sustained "serious injuries" and was in a "critical but stable" condition at Addenbrooke's Hospital in Cambridge.

entries.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
entries.description: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

entries.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
entries.description: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

entries.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets
entries.description: New York erupted in jubilant scenes on Saturday when the Knicks broke a 53-year drought to defeat the San Antonio Spurs away in Texas.

entries.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
entries.description: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

entries.title: Péter Magyar's EU summit debut signals Hungarian reset after change of guard
entries.description: Magyar was met with optimism at his first EU summit in Brussels, while Orbán rallied far-right allies nearby at much smaller venue urging him to veto the bloc's next seven-year budget. For the institutions, the Orbán era is over.

entries.title: An EU of six, not 27, best way to 'reinforce Europe', Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews
entries.description: The former French finance minister argued that new impetus should be given to the European Union, namely by uniting six core countries to deliver on key issues in the face of foreign threats.

entries.title: Costa defends surprise decision to open diplomatic channel with Russia
entries.description: António Costa’s surprise decision to open a diplomatic channel with Russia dominated discussions at Thursday’s summit, as several leaders expressed concern that they had not been consulted before the call was launched.

entries.title: Ukraine deserves fast EU membership ‘more than any other country,’ Zelenskyy says
entries.description: Zelenskyy called on EU leaders to open all accession clusters in the coming weeks and eventually endorse Kyiv’s fast-track membership bid, saying it "deserves full EU membership more than any other European country”.

entries.title: Hegseth announces review of US forces in Europe as he lambasts NATO allies in Brussels meeting
entries.description: Hegseth slammed Europe for prioritising what he called liberal ideals over practical defence needs as he lambasted the alliance’s defence chiefs in a rare appearance at the NATO HQ in Brussels.

entries.title: Timelapse video shows ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz since reopening
entries.description: A timelapse shows vessels moving through the Strait of Hormuz after the US and Iran signed an agreement ending their conflict and reopening one of the world's most important shipping routes.

entries.title: From oak forests to steppe lakes: Central Europe's five best summer destinations
entries.description: Bored of the crowded seasides, the heat island big cities? Visit Central Europe's lakes, zoos and other exciting places that can unite all generations.

entries.title: EU leaders to hold talks on common budget at Brussels summit as battle for cash begins
entries.description: EU governments will hold substantial negotiations on how to allocate €2 trillion of the European common budget over the next seven years, pitting frugals calling for cuts against member states defending money for agriculture to be preserved in tough talks ahead.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 19th, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 19th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: W thinks it has the X Factor: Europe’s alternative to mainstream social media
entries.description: The Swedish-based “W” platform” is the latest in a series of new social media sites vying to replace US Big Tech companies.

entries.title: Mexico secures ticket to knockout stages as Canada breeze past Qatar with ruthless 6-0 win
entries.description: Mexico became the first team to secure a knockout rounds ticket after beating South Korea 1-0 to top Group A with six points on matchday two of the group stages.

entries.title: EU leaders debate Ukraine as Costa opens diplomatic channel with Kremlin
entries.description: EU leaders wrapped up talks in Brussels after President António Costa opened a diplomatic channel with the Kremlin. Volodymyr Zelenskyy renewed his call for fast-track EU membership, while Hungary's new Prime Minister Péter Magyar made his European Council debut.

entries.title: JD Vance cancels trip to Switzerland for planned US-Iran talks
entries.description: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an initial agreement aimed at bringing the Iran war to a close on Wednesday.

entries.title: EU leaders kick off heated talks on €2 trillion long-term budget plan
entries.description: The EU's 27 leaders have kicked off their second day of talks in Brussels. On the table, the divisive issue of the next long-term budget, which is splitting EU governments into two opposing camps.

entries.title: Newsletter: EU leaders poised for battle over cash
entries.description: Also in this newsletter: EU leaders react to European Council President António Costa laying the groundwork for diplomatic contacts with Russia; and exclusive comments from Sweden's EU affairs minister as EU leaders gear up to debate the bloc's next long-term budget.

entries.title: Battle of the branding: Who is competing for inaugural European CMO of the Year at Cannes Lions
entries.description: From L'Oréal and Ferrari, to LEGO and Gymshark, 29 senior marketing leaders are competing for the inaugural European Chief Marketing Officer of the Year award at Cannes Lions.

entries.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
entries.description: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

entries.title: Andy Burnham wins key UK by-election, setting up Labour leadership battle with Starmer
entries.description: In his victory speech, Burnham warned the Labour Party that it had a "final chance to change".

entries.title: A cosmic tribute: Asteroid named after beloved singer Elliott Smith
entries.description: More than two decades after his death, Elliott Smith has been immortalized with an asteroid named after him. The celestial honour came from a fan listening to his song ‘Shooting Star’...

entries.title: 'All of Lebanon must burn,' Israeli minister says after IDF reports four soldiers killed
entries.description: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

entries.title: Podcast | Ten years on, Brexit ghosts still linger
entries.description: Ten years ago, the UK voted to leave the European Union. On the eve of the 23 June anniversary, we look at how both the EU and the UK have changed since Brexit.

entries.title: Farms, schools, rail and health services under strain as heatwave grips France
entries.description: With temperatures set to soar as high as 40°C in the coming days, many workers are already changing their routines to adapt to the imminent challenges. In Bordeaux’s vineyards, the working day now starts before dawn as to protect workers from peak climates during the middle of the day.

entries.title: Europe looks at banning social media for teens, and girls are most impacted
entries.description: As the UK joins a growing list of countries looking at banning or severely curbing children's access to social media, data shows girls have higher levels of problematic social media use than boys across Europe.

entries.title: Football not covered by US-Iran framework deal, Iran's World Cup squad finds
entries.description: Just two days after Washington and Tehran signed a framework deal aimed at ending their war, Iran's football federation said US travel restrictions on its World Cup squad remain unchanged, and is filing a formal complaint with FIFA.

entries.title: Reprezentacja Iranu: porozumienie USA–Iran nie obejmuje piłki nożnej

entries.title: Russian drones hit two foreign-flagged civilian ships in the Black Sea, Ukraine says
entries.description: Local air defences shot down 79 of 90 Russian drones from Thursday evening into Friday morning, per Ukraine's air force.

entries.title: Obama Presidential Centre opens with star-studded ceremony and veiled jabs at Trump
entries.description: On stage, Obama voiced his support for character, honesty, integrity, kindness, compassion and sense of duty, praising both Democrats and Republicans.

entries.title: The screams through the wall: the hidden toll of Russia's war against Ukraine
entries.description: On the International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict, Jacqueline Mahon, UNFPA Representative to Ukraine, writes in a Euronews op-ed that wartime sexual violence scars survivors, families and communities, while funding cuts threaten support services.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 19th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 19th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Uzbekistan celebrates first-ever World Cup goal despite defeat to Colombia
entries.description: The Central Asian country's World Cup debut brought early-morning crowds, large public screenings and a memorable goal that many supporters considered more important than the final score.

entries.title: Iranian artist Parastoo Ahmadi reportedly sentenced to 74 lashes for singing without hijab
entries.description: Iranian singer Parastoo Ahmadi has been reportedly sentenced to 74 lashes for singing without hijab. For Iranian artists, the ruling deepens fears of escalating cultural repression.

entries.title: From Pompeii to Évora : invisible solar for heritage sites
entries.description: Solar panels disguised as ancient Roman tiles or designed to blend into historic skylines. Pompeii and Évora are proving that heritage preservation and sustainability can go hand in hand.

entries.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
entries.description: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

entries.title: Who is the most evil Disney villain? The answer may surprise you
entries.description: The House of Mouse has given audiences some cracking scoundrels over the years. Some heinous, some nightmarish, and some deeply misunderstood. And one is especially irredeemable...

entries.title: US-Iran deal is 'very fragile' as conflict 'remains', says analyst
entries.description: Tomi Huhtanen, Executive Director of the Wilfried Martens Centre, believes the EU should be "involved" in the peace deal. Huhtanen said the bloc needed to be "in the same group chat" when discussing potential contacts with Russia, arguing that coordination among European countries "has not worked".

entries.title: Cypriot minister vows to keep 'building' on EU accession momentum
entries.description: As the last days of Cyprus’s rotating EU presidency approach, Deputy Minister for European Affairs, Marilena Raouna, says her country will work "very hard" to open the remaining phases of talks on Ukraine and Moldova's EU membership.

entries.title: Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as Argentina captain’s father faces health issues
entries.description: The family of Argentina national football team captain Lionel Messi has issued a public statement confirming that his father, Jorge Messi, “is going through a health situation,” amid rumors about Jorge Messi's health. The family asked for “responsibility, prudence and humanity.”

entries.title: Rodzina Messiego apeluje o człowieczeństwo, ojciec chory
entries.description: Rodzina Lionela Messiego, kapitana reprezentacji Argentyny, poinformowała, że jego ojciec Jorge ma problemy zdrowotne, i wezwała do odpowiedzialności, rozwagi i człowieczeństwa.

entries.title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz amid reports Iran has reclosed the waterway

entries.title: Estonia creates AI ‘ID Codes’ to govern autonomous agents
entries.description: The government says AI ID codes will make it easier for companies and individuals to automate work without giving an agent access to all their data.

entries.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
entries.description: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

entries.title: Robin Hood’s famed Sherwood Forest 1,200-year-old oak tree declared dead

entries.title: Giorgia Meloni fires back at Trump's 'completely made-up claims'
entries.description: The Italian PM said on Friday that she was "shocked" at Trump's comments. The once close pair's relationship has been increasingly strained in recent months.

entries.title: Von der Leyen told EU leaders it's right time to consider a mandate for talks with Russia
entries.description: European Commission President von der Leyen told EU leaders it is time to be prepared and consider an EU mandate to negotiate with Russia on Ukraine, as Costa's controversial Kremlin outreach draws pushback.

entries.title: Game changer sweets: the Bavarian start-up making sustainable ‘chocolate’ from sunflowers
entries.description: Do you have a sweet tooth? Let’s dig into the world’s first cocoa-free chocolate alternative made from sunflower seeds. ‘Made in Europe’ learns the secret behind ChoViva, a Bavarian newcomer in the food industry.

entries.title: Matisse and Yves Saint Laurent come together in "Beauty, Fashion and Happiness" exhibition

entries.title: Is it now safe to travel to the Middle East? What to know as UK downgrades advisories
entries.description: The new advice makes it easier for British holidaymakers to travel to destinations like Dubai and Doha.

entries.title: Time to bring back shame? Olivia Rodrigo reveals she has smelled diaper-wearing fans at live shows

entries.title: Would you pay €50 to visit Venice? New mayor is pushing for entry fee hike
entries.description: The council is also evaluating whether to extend the fee to the entire year, or perhaps include more dates.

entries.title: ‘We need to talk about this,’ Kallas-Israel spat draws mixed reactions from EU leaders
entries.description: EU's foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas faced a mixed reaction at the Brussels summit over alleged remarks comparing Israel to apartheid South Africa, after Israel cut contact with her.

entries.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The returning MP set to challenge Keir Starmer

entries.title: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew ceasefire, officials say
entries.description: Lebanese media reported an Israeli airstrike in Southern Lebanon on Friday, after a US official said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed on a ceasefire.

entries.title: Airbus at Vivatech: How quantum sensing and AI can transform flying

entries.title: EU Commission to develop diversification instrument, von der Leyen says

ABE Uta made history with a record-breaking 12th Grand Slam title, while KOGA Wakana, TAKEOKA Takeshi and OMORI Akari also struck gold. AGHAYEV Balabay defeated world champion NAGAYAMA Ryuju for -60kg gold.

entries.title: Sánchez backs international dialogue against other leaders: 'A Europe open to the world'
entries.description: Spain’s prime minister used the European Council summit to urge a more ambitious multiannual budget, and voiced solidarity with Meloni after Trump said she begged him for a photo.

entries.title: Jordan Bardella meets Karol Nawrocki in Warsaw
entries.description: French far‑right leader Jordan Bardella on Thursday began a two‑day visit to Poland, seeking meetings with right‑wing and nationalist figures ahead of next year’s French presidential election, after similar trips to Portugal, Italy and Belgium.

entries.title: Tired of jet lag? Experts share their top tips for travel recovery
entries.description: Jet lag can derail any trip, but experts say careful planning before, during and after a flight can help travellers adjust more quickly.

entries.description: Jordan Bardella, leader of France's far-right National Rally, said he would like to "put the Commission and the EU back at the service of the nations and no longer the other way around".

entries.title: Forget 'Do Not Touch': Choreographer William Forsythe invites you to dance with the exhibits

entries.description: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

entries.description: It comes after Israel carried out a wave of fresh strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday morning.

entries.title: Judo: Home nation strikes gold in Ulaanbaatar

World champions Haruka Kaju and Shiho Tanaka added more gold for Japan, while Korea’s Lee Joonhwan impressed with a spectacular ippon to claim the -81kg title.

entries.title: Wife of Spanish PM Pedro Pedro Sánchez has passport revoked as court orders corruption trial

entries.title: From Évian to Brussels: Inside a whirlwind week of high-stakes diplomacy

entries.description: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each to power Netherlands to a 5-1 win over Sweden to bounce back after a disappointing draw in its opener and move atop the Group F standings.

entries.title: From ruin to restoration: Byzantine Greek Monastery becomes 'Art Reliquary' for spiritual works

entries.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

entries.title: Fête de la Musique 2026: When the whole of France becomes one big music festival
entries.description: If you are out and about anywhere in France on 21 June 2026, your ears will be in for a treat as the 45th edition of the now world-famous music event "Fête de la musique" takes place. The lineup country-wide is very promising...

entries.title: This cave village in France offers a natural escape from extreme heat

entries.title: Japan fans keep World Cup clean-up tradition alive

Supporters collected rubbish in the stadium before leaving, a gesture that has drawn admiration at every World Cup since 1998.

entries.title: Poland and Ukraine's ‘honours war’ intensifies

entries.title: Rare medieval notebook found in latrine in Paderborn
entries.description: Archaeologists have uncovered a medieval notebook in a former latrine in Paderborn. The remarkably well-preserved leather, wood and wax find may shed new light on everyday life in the 13th and 14th centuries.

entries.title: Rare medieval notebook found in Paderborn latrine
entries.description: Archaeologists have unearthed a medieval notebook in a former latrine in Paderborn. The remarkably well-preserved leather, wood and wax find may shed new light on daily life in the 13th and 14th centuries.

entries.title: Zanzibar travel guide: Best things to do, where to stay and top tips for visitors
entries.description: Tanzania's island paradise has emerged as one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing travel destinations.

entries.title: France bans alcohol at Fête de la Musique as heatwave grips Europe
entries.description: Sunday is the summer solstice, meaning the sun is at its strongest and UV levels will be high across the continent.

entries.title: Mundial: Holandia gromi Szwecję, Niemcy unikają wielkiej wpadki
entries.description: Brian Brobbey i Cody Gakpo strzelili po dwa gole, a Holandia rozbiła Szwecję 5:1, rehabilitując się po rozczarowującym remisie w swoim meczu otwarcia.

entries.title: These Gulf airlines are offering insurance to entice passengers back to the Middle East
entries.description: Both Emirates and Etihad Airways have announced new insurance schemes which will protect travellers whose countries still list the region as a no-go zone.

entries.title: Starmer reflecting on 'political realities' amid reports of imminent resignation
entries.description: The British PM has repeatedly vowed to fight on in his role but has come under increasing pressure in recent weeks.

entries.title: A Spectacular Finish to the Ulaanbaatar Grand Slam
entries.description: The Ulaanbaatar Grand Slam concluded with outstanding performances from the world’s top judoka. MURAO Sanshiro claimed a ninth Grand Slam title, LYTVYNENKO Yelyzaveta triumphed in -78kg, while PIRELLI Gennaro, ARAI Mao and KIM Minjong secured gold on a thrilling final day in Mongolia.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 21st, 2026 – Evening

entries.title: Drone show in Matosinhos breaks Guinness record

entries.title: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
entries.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

entries.title: James Burrows, director of beloved sitcom "Friends", dies aged 85
entries.description: James Burrows won 11 Emmy awards during his career and worked as director, producer and writer in more than 1,000 episodes of hit TV programmes.

entries.title: Spain clinch their first win of the tournament in 4-0 drubbing of Saudi Arabia

entries.title: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
entries.description: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts  over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

entries.title: Major explosion at Qatar's largest energy site injures dozens as 18 remain unaccounted for
entries.description: Qatar’s Interior Ministry says search and rescue operations are continuing to locate close to two dozen people who remain missing following the large explosion at the country’s main energy site.

entries.title: Kazakhstan and the European Union: Building the next chapter of a strategic partnership
entries.description: The partnership between Kazakhstan and the EU is more important today than ever before, writes President of the Republic of Kazakhstan Kassym-Jomart Tokayev in an opinion article for Euronews, identifying three key strategic objectives to build the next chapter of a strategic partnership.

entries.title: Mandarin Oriental is set to return to this southeast Asian capital this winter
entries.description: Luxury hospitality brand will return to Manila after a 14-year absence, bringing with it suites with skyline views and a wellness floor inspired by Filipino traditions.

entries.title: Supergirl, Frida Kahlo and dragons: What to see, do or hear this week in Europe
entries.description: A major new Frida Kahlo exhibition arrives in London, Supergirl blasts onto the big screen, and House of the Dragon returns for a third season. Here's what to look out for this week.

entries.title: How does the EU ensure pay transparency? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot
entries.description: The EU is rewriting compensation rules. Member states had until 7 June to implement the Pay Transparency Directive, which addresses pay transparency and gender pay gap. But what does it change in practice? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot.

entries.title: Europe Today: Starmer set to step down as US-Iran talks and Moldova summit unfold
entries.description: US-Iran talks continued after a temporary pause threatened the fragile agreement, as Brussels hosted the second EU-Moldova summit focused on Chişinău's membership bid. Meanwhile, pressure grew on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer as tensions between Poland and Ukraine continued to escalate.

entries.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup
entries.description: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

entries.title: 'Toy Story 5' breaks record and scores biggest box office debut of 2026
entries.description: Pixar’s Toy Story franchise has proven its enduring appeal, with the fifth instalment shattering box office records and securing the biggest opening weekend of 2026.

entries.title: Newsletter: A Commissioner's secret trip to Israel
entries.description: In this newsletter: A high-ranking EU official visits Jerusalem on Monday, exposing a vacuum of information about the trip; UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to lay-out how he will vacate Number 10 Downing Street; plus, the EU-Moldova summit gets underway in the Belgian capital.

entries.title: Can the EU fix the gender pay gap? Take our poll.
entries.description: The gender pay gap a persistent problem in Europe, where men earn, on average 12.7 percent more per hour. The EU has just implemented its new Pay Transparency Directive, to pull the curtain back on salary secrecy and empower employees.

entries.title: BABELL turns Porto into a 'book city' with internationally renowned authors
entries.description: Two Nobel laureates, Booker Prize winners and writers will make Porto a literary capital from 24 to 29 June at the BABELL Festival, to bring readers closer to bookshops.

entries.title: Australian police seize nearly 3 tonnes of cocaine in what has become the largest bust on record
entries.description: The bust of nearly 3 tonnes is the country’s largest ever and is believed to hold a street value of just shy of €500 million.

entries.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row
entries.description: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

entries.title: Venice to Istanbul: Explore more of the Balkans on the Golden Eagle Danube Express
entries.description: Golden Eagle Luxury Trains has refreshed its ‘Balkan Explorer’ rail route with an overnight stay in Belgrade, new Croatian stops and extended time in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

entries.title: China announces sanctions on 10 US companies as trade tensions flare
entries.description: The sanctions come a month after US President Donald Trump visited Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping aimed at stabilising relations.

entries.title: Daft Punk’s ‘One More Time’ becomes French team’s official 2026 World Cup goal song
entries.description: Les Bleus’ next World Cup game is set for tonight against Iraq, and their chosen soundtrack is already delighting music fans...

entries.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigns as Andy Burnham announces candidacy
entries.description: Starmer will continue as caretaker prime minister until a new Labour leader is elected, while Reform UK has demanded a general election.

entries.title: Cape Verde produce another World Cup shock to keep remarkable run alive

entries.title: A decade later: Europeans more positive about the EU than during Brexit
entries.description: Polling from Pew Research shows favourable views of the EU have increased since Brexit, even as national polling indicates support for some eurosceptic parties is at a high.

entries.title: The party is over: Why France is looking to tighten its grip on free parties
entries.description: On Monday, the French National Assembly will start reviewing a new governmental bill aiming to crack down on unauthorised outdoor gatherings, or 'free parties'.

entries.title: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
entries.description: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 22nd, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 22nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Moldova's EU accession and Transnistria settlement are 'parallel processes', minister says
entries.description: Moldovan Foreign Minister Mihai Popșoi said the country's EU accession process and the Transnistria conflict are "parallel" tracks to prevent Vladimir Putin from gaining "leverage". Moldova is working "tirelessly" to ensure all Moldovans benefit from accession, said the minister.

entries.title: 10 years of Brexit: Which campaign claims have come true?
entries.description: A decade on from the referendum that led to the UK leaving the European Union, Euronews' fact-checking team looks at the campaign promises that were made on both the Leave and Remain sides to see which ones ended up happening.

entries.title: Octola III: Embrace Arctic ultra-luxury at this invitation-only retreat in Finnish Lapland
entries.description: Opening in December, this private lodge accommodates just six guests and access is granted exclusively through an invitation-only waiting list.

entries.title: Sesame Street scandal: Elmo’s World Cup support reveals Republican ‘hypocrisy’
entries.description: A wholesome clip of Sesame Street's Elmo saying he is supporting Team USA during the FIFA World Cup has gone viral, and the House Republicans could not resist posting a reply... It has not gone down well.

entries.title: Assouline launches first-ever travel series kiosk at Doha's Hamad International Airport
entries.description: Travellers passing through Doha will be able to buy books, candles, totes and other gifts from the luxury publisher.

entries.title: Spain's Supreme Court jails former socialist minister for 24 years in COVID corruption case
entries.description: A major blow to Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, Spain's Supreme Court has sentenced former socialist minister and close Sánchez ally José Luis Ábalos to 24 years in prison in a corruption case linked to COVID-19 mask contracts.

entries.title: Kazakhstan's President in Brussels: Focus on energy, transportation routes, AI, and more
entries.description: Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev is visiting Brussels. His goal: to deepen cooperation between the EU and Kazakhstan. The 73-year-old previously outlined what this might look like in an op-ed for Euronews. We have summarized the key points.

entries.title: The only way to get good at AI is to fail at it, says AWS’s chief marketing officer Julia White
entries.description: "AI is a wonderful thought partner — but it's not a tastemaker," says AWS marketing chief Julie White in an interview with Euronews Next.

entries.title: EU's cloud and AI development act gets mixed reception
entries.description: The European Commission's goal of tripling the EU’s data centre market within five to seven years would not be possible without intrusive market engineering, and CADA does exactly that.

entries.title: Unijna ustawa o rozwoju chmury i AI budzi mieszane reakcje
entries.description: Cel Komisji Europejskiej, by w ciągu 5–7 lat potroić unijny rynek centrów danych, wymaga agresywnej inżynierii rynku – i właśnie tym zajmuje się CADA.

entries.title: SZA hits out at 'disgusting' AI music after discovering her songs were used in AI training
entries.description: "If you’re a musician and you support this degenerate shit? You’re disgusting.” The award-winning R&B singer has hit out at AI and Suno, which has maintained that its models don’t store or reproduce specific songs.

entries.title: Inside Ukraine’s campaign to turn Russian-occupied Crimea into an 'island'

entries.title: Canal Saint-Martin becomes cooling spot as heatwave grips Paris

entries.title: Epidaurus: 10,000-strong ovation for National Opera's 'Medea'

entries.title: 'Passeport' controversy explained: Why did a French town cancel a play about refugees?
entries.description: A mayor's decision to cancel a play about refugees has caused uproar in France and raised concerns about the far-right's grip on culture.

entries.title: Who is Abelardo de la Espriella? From controversial lawyer to Colombia's president

entries.title: Giant dragon and octopus kites fill Danish skies

entries.title: Iran and US make progress on Hormuz despite Trump's explosive threats

entries.title: Holiday islands under pressure: The snake invasion threatening Ibiza and Mallorca

entries.title: EU's phase-out of China-made inverters gets a reality check

entries.title: Greece records strong rise in tourism revenue and arrivals in first four months of 2026

entries.title: Pope Leo XIV at World Food Programme: "conflicts are ‘fed’ more readily than people are nourished"

entries.title: Israel FM urges EU dialogue, rejects ‘dictated’ positions after Kallas row

entries.title: EU 're-assessing' summit with UK after Keir Starmer lays out exit plan
entries.description: A European Commission spokesperson said on Monday that the EU is "reassessing" plans to hold a summit with the UK in late July, as it becomes increasingly likely that Prime Minister Keir Starmer's successor will be in post in just three and a half weeks.

entries.title: 'Another day, another leader': Londoners react to Starmer's resignation

entries.description: The statement was circulated to editorial offices late on Monday night, reaching newsrooms shortly before midnight, according to the organisation.

entries.description: To jedna z największych bomb transferowych w historii NBA. Według Associated Press Giannis Antetokounmpo po 13 latach ma opuścić Milwaukee Bucks i przenieść się do Miami Heat.

entries.description: Po remisie na otwarcie Portugalia jest pod presją przed meczem z ekipą Fabio Cannavaro, stawiającą na mocną obronę i atak. W składzie znów Rúben Dias i prawdopodobnie Ronaldo, mimo słabego występu kapitana w pierwszym spotkaniu.

entries.title: 2026 edition of Edinburgh International Festival to be a ‘phone-free’ environment

entries.title: TV pundit apologises after racist comment during Belgium World Cup game
entries.description: Bogdanović most notably played for La Liga side Atlético Madrid and Germany's Werder Bremen during his career.

entries.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea

entries.title: Pay transparency isn't only about women. It exposes inequalities affecting millions of workers
entries.description: The EU's pay transparency rules can help close the gender pay gap and expose how Europe systematically underpays young, migrant, disabled and racialised workers.

entries.title: Fact check: Viral drone interception video shows Russia's Yolka system
entries.description: A video widely shared on social media appears to show a handheld device intercepting a drone. While some users have hailed the system as a Ukrainian military innovation, others have claimed it is Russian technology. The Cube traced the footage to determine the origin and purpose of the device.

entries.title: Pro-Israel Spanish journalist Pilar Rahola accused of complicity in genocide
entries.description: Barcelona’s public prosecutor has opened proceedings against Rahola over allegations of incitement to hatred and complicity in genocide.

entries.title: Ukraine gets €3.2 billion from EU support loan, von der Leyen says
entries.description: The announcement marks the first disbursement under the €90 billion loan. A second payment worth about €6 billion and dedicated to drone production is expected to be unveiled in the coming days.

entries.title: Eleven EU countries call for methane rules pause amid energy security fears
entries.description: The eleven EU countries say that the methane rules could jeopardise natural gas supply, especially from foreign suppliers who would face extra costs.  Environmentalists warn the EU not to cave in to pressure while the United Nations urged world nations to do more to mitigate methane emissions.

entries.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
entries.description: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Why are Boston bars running out of Scottish beer?
entries.description: Boston bars say Scottish fans have sparked a shortage of Tennents Lager during the World Cup, with some pubs running low as thousands of supporters descend on the city. One bar says it may be the last in Boston still serving the popular Scottish beer.

entries.title: Qatar fans hopeful about the future despite World Cup exit after Bosnia defeat
entries.description: Qatar's World Cup dream came to an end after three group matches, with fans in Doha reflecting on the team's strong performance while remaining optimistic about the future, as Qatar emerges from the war with hope.

entries.title: Macron says France intercepted a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker off coast of Sicily
entries.description: It comes after the UK announced earlier this month that it had intercepted and boarded the Russian shadow fleet tanker "Smyrtos" in the English Channel.

entries.title: US-Iran talks could derail fragile peace process, says ex-Israeli spokesperson
entries.description: Former Israeli government spokesperson Eylon Levy believes Israel's campaign is "creating the urgency and opportunity" for Lebanon to confront Hezbollah. Levy said that if Lebanon is "capable of dismantling Hezbollah", it could mark the beginning of "a beautiful peace between Israel and Lebanon.

entries.title: Phoebe Bridgers announces new album after solo hiatus
entries.description: The American singer is coming back with her third solo record 'Lost Weekend' ahead of a US and European tour starting in September.

entries.title: SpaceX has quietly launched a spacecraft almost no one knew existed
entries.description: Rumours have circulated that it could be used to transport military hardware.

entries.title: SpaceX po cichu wynosi w kosmos statek, o którym prawie nikt nie wiedział
entries.description: Pojawiły się pogłoski, że może służyć do transportu sprzętu wojskowego.

entries.title: Mediterranean travel trends: How ‘blue mind experiences’ and local culture keep people coming back
entries.description: As fly-and-flop holidays fall out of fashion, travellers are looking for more meaningful travel experiences even while prioritising sun and sea.

entries.title: Europe summer travel trends: Short breaks, family holidays and coolcations drive demand
entries.description: From quick getaways to heatwave-avoiding escapes, new data from Trip.com reveals the trends that are shaping summer travel across Europe in 2026.

entries.title: Bigger than Jupiter, lighter than cotton candy: Astronomers discover two ‘super-puff’ planets
entries.description: The two planets orbiting a star 1,110 light-years away are the biggest exoplanets ever found with a density lower than cotton candy, and may be mostly made of hydrogen and helium.

entries.title: Większe od Jowisza, lżejsze niż wata cukrowa: odkryto dwie planety superpuffy
entries.description: Dwie planety krążące wokół gwiazdy odległej o 1110 lat świetlnych to największe znane egzoplanety o gęstości mniejszej niż wata cukrowa; prawdopodobnie składają się głównie z wodoru i helu.

entries.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
entries.description: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

entries.title: Kibice Kataru patrzą w przyszłość z nadzieją mimo odpadnięcia po porażce z Bośnią
entries.description: Mundialowy sen Kataru zakończył się po trzech meczach grupowych. Kibice w Dosze chwalą postawę drużyny i z nadzieją patrzą w przyszłość, gdy kraj wychodzi z wojny.

entries.title: How much do we really know about microplastics? | Euronews Tech Talks
entries.description: Microplastics pollute our planet, raising questions about their impact on human health and the environment. This Q&A episode of Euronews Tech Talks sums up what science knows, and does not know, about them.

entries.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
entries.description: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

entries.title: Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup is taking place in Kazakhstan
entries.description: Kazakhstan’s Zhetysu region is hosting the first Paragliding World Cup in Central Asia, bringing together around 150 pilots from 33 countries. The main discipline of the World Cup is cross-country flying.

entries.title: Leonard Cohen estate slams use of 'Hallelujah' at Trump's Freedom 250 rally
entries.description: Donald Trump had announced earlier in June that 'Hallelujah' would be performed at the opening of the Great American State Fair, fuelling the long-term feud between the US president and the estate of Leonard Cohen.

entries.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
entries.description: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

entries.title: Laissons Parler Les Gens: Podcast addresses issues facing young Africans online
entries.description: Youssoupha acknowledged cyberbullying and harassment were part of the challenges facing young Africans today, but he urged the youth to focus on the positives.

entries.title: James Bond search enters final stage with planned August screen tests
entries.description: Production on Bond 26 is set to begin next year. The expectation is that the official announcement for the next 007 actor will happen by the end of 2026.

entries.title: Brussels tightens rules on US cloud providers as it pushes for tech sovereignty
entries.description: The European Commission has decided that Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web Services should face strict rules to improve cloud competition. The move risks angering Washington as Brussels pushes the European tech sovereignty agenda.

entries.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
entries.description: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

entries.title: 'I'm in great shape,' boxing legend Floyd Mayweather tells Euronews ahead of Athens fight
entries.description: Floyd "Money" Mayweather is set to face Greek fighter Mike Zambidis in Athens on Saturday 27 June.

entries.title: NATO chief Rutte defends allies as Trump says he was 'let down' on Iran
entries.description: Rutte pushed back gently against Trump's latest NATO complaints, saying, “I know there have been isolated cases about which you are really disappointed, but generally speaking, your European allies have been there with you."

entries.title: Heat stress threatens 130 million European workers, trade union body warns
entries.description: The European Trade Union Institute (ETUI) has presented a proposal for European legislation to protect workers' health during heatwaves.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Evening

entries.title: Floyd Mayweather Jr. o karierze, formie i sukcesie. Ekskluzywny wywiad dla Euronews
entries.description: Legenda boksu zawodowego Floyd Mayweather Jr. przed walką w Atenach udzielił ekskluzywnego wywiadu dla Euronews. Amerykanin opowiedział o swojej formie, karierze i motywacji do dalszych występów.

entries.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning

entries.title: Football for friends: Winning ways World Cup supporters have gone viral – for the right reasons

entries.title: EU's door 'is open to UK', fomer Brexit negotiator Barnier tells Euronews
entries.description: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

entries.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians

entries.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 235, search and rescue efforts continue
entries.description: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

entries.title: Europe’s Taliban Dilemma: the struggle to return Afghan migrants
entries.description: EU countries are engaging with Afghanistan’s Taliban-led regime to return irregular migrants deemed security threats or convicted of serious offences. Yet any such arrangement could carry political and diplomatic costs, requiring concessions and risking public backlash.

entries.title: Côte d’Ivoire: Discover West Africa’s beach and culture hotspot
entries.description: Regarded as West Africa’s answer to the Caribbean, Côte d’Ivoire has an extraordinary coast, UNESCO-listed culture and direct flights from Europe.

entries.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
entries.description: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Supergirl' - Save the puppy, save the world
entries.description: After a strong start to the rebooted DC Universe last year with 'Superman' comes  a new space adventure which follows the Man of Steel's chaotic cousin... And her super-powered pup.

entries.title: How Portugal's Alentejo region has become a hotspot for luxury hotel openings

entries.title: Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge dropped - accuser says she can't endure a fourth trial
entries.description: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California. He remains behind bars at New York's maximum-security prison, Rikers Island.

entries.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash

entries.title: UK foreign policy to remain unchanged despite government transition, deputy PM Lammy says

entries.title: Turkey produces last minute winner to beat the US as Germany end win streak with loss to Ecuador
entries.description: The final matchday saw teams take on both more cautious and reckless approaches as they looked to secure qualification into the knockout stages.

entries.title: Tehran will allow nuclear inspections during peace talks, former Iranian diplomat says

entries.title: Amid shifts in energy supply, which EU countries consume and produce the most gas?

entries.title: Peppa Pig backlash as US company Hasbro requires child actors to sign voices over to AI

An open letter signed by 1,000 industry professionals condemns the AI contract clause and warns of matters of consent.

entries.title: Ukraine launches 40-day operation to ‘pressure Russia to end the war’
entries.description: After several weeks of a strike campaign against energy and military targets in Moscow-occupied Crimea, Ukraine is now launching a 40-day operation aimed at “pressuring Russia to end its war”.

entries.description: Inspectors discovered an additional 2,100 tonnes of textile waste stored in a warehouse linked to a Turkish recycling facility that was noncompliant with environmental laws. Meanwhile, France is urging EU countries to get tough on ultra-fast fashion.

entries.description: The Greek astronaut will conduct experiments and technology demonstrations proposed by dozens of Greek research teams, institutions and universities.

entries.title: 'Measures needed to face extreme heat or it will kill us,' Copernicus director says
entries.description: As scorching weather hits Europe, climate expert Carlo Buontempo told Euronews' flagship morning show Europe Today that "record-breaking temperatures should not be breaking news" and that strategies are needed to face extreme weather lest humanity suffers the consequences.

entries.description: The report ranked European countries for their abundance of lakes rated as “excellent”, meaning their water quality was safe for swimming.

Hayato Kondo claimed a second Qingdao title, Kokoro Fujishiro earned her first Grand Prix gold, Hifumi Abe added another title, Maysa Pardayeva celebrated a maiden World Tour victory.

entries.title: EU countries move to revive temporary message-scanning regime, but it could backfire

entries.description: There have already been calls to put the system on hold and airports requiring extra security to manage queues.

entries.description: Portugal may be taking a decisive step in the global race for stealth, with a graphene-based radar-absorbing material that could render drones and warplanes almost undetectable.

entries.title: Z laboratorium w chmury: portugalski grafen ukrywa samoloty i drony, rewolucjonizuje obronę
entries.description: Portugalia może wykonać przełomowy krok w globalnym wyścigu technologii stealth: opracowuje grafenowy materiał pochłaniający fale radarowe, który sprawi, że drony i samoloty wojskowe będą niemal niewykrywalne.

entries.title: Rare Strawberry Moon shines over Lisbon
entries.description: The strawberry moon rises over Lisbon, drawing crowds to viewpoints across the city as locals and tourists watch the rare moon above the River Tagus.

entries.title: 'C'mon queen!': Naomi Osaka wows Wimbledon crowd with ‘Kill Bill’ inspired kimono
entries.description: Japan's four-time major winner paid tribute to her nation's culture by arriving for her opening match at Wimbledon in a full-length kimono - entirely white to adhere to the tournament's strict dress code rules.

entries.title: Watch: Why Serbia’s most powerful politican just quit the presidency
entries.description: Last weekend, President Aleksandar Vučić stunned the nation by announcing he will resign within weeks and call early presidential and parliamentary elections. However, do not mistake this for a total surrender.

entries.title: What are the most liveable European cities in the world?
entries.description: Urban populations continue to surge worldwide, bringing new opportunities and challenges. So which European cities stand out for quality of life in 2026?

entries.title: Robert Lewandowski zmienia klub. Chicago Fire stawia na polską gwiazdę
entries.description: Po tygodniach spekulacji wszystko jest już jasne. Robert Lewandowski przenosi się do Major League Soccer i będzie reprezentował barwy Chicago Fire. Tym samym zamyka czteroletni etap gry w Barcelonie i rozpoczyna nowy rozdział swojej kariery.

entries.title: European rescue teams arrive in Venezuela in desperate bid to find quake survivors
entries.description: Tens of thousands of people remain unaccounted for and time is running out to find survivors following the quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5, the strongest to hit the South American nation in more than a century.

entries.title: EU ends tax loophole exploited by SHEIN, Temu, and Aliexpress
entries.description: The European Union is imposing a new tax on small imports, which will heavily impact Chinese giants like SHEIN, Temu, and AliExpress. What does this mean for European consumers, product quality, and brands?

entries.title: Feliks the eagle back home in Serbia after kidnap and illegal sale ordeal in Middle East
entries.description: Feliks' ordeal highlighted both the widespread practice of profit-driven, illegal animal trade and an unfaltering struggle by animal protection groups to counter it.

entries.title: Mistrzostwa Świata 2026. Niemcy i Holandia odpadają po karnych, Brazylia wygrywa w końcówce
entries.description: Poniedziałkowe mecze fazy pucharowej MŚ przyniosły sensacje: Paragwaj i Maroko ograły po karnych Niemcy i Holandię, a Brazylia w końcówce pokonała Japonię.

entries.title: In the rough: Critics outraged at Donald Trump’s latest golf project
entries.description: Donald Trump has announced his new plan: the renovation of Washington D.C.'s East Potomac Golf Links. Critics aren't happy about the "vanity project".

entries.title: Venezuela earthquake: La Guaira port turned into makeshift morgue
entries.description: La Guaira port in Venezuela is turned into a makeshift morgue after twin earthquakes, with forensic teams processing victims and families waiting for news.

entries.title: France to light up Statue of Liberty for US 250th anniversary
entries.description: The famous statue inviting the world to send “your tired, your poor… your huddled masses yearning to live free” was built in France; for America’s 250th, France is preparing a monumental artwork.

entries.title: Hot dogs in first class? Fast food classic goes gourmet on Swiss First’s in-flight menu
entries.description: The ‘bastardo’ hot dog will be available in SWISS First on all flights departing Switzerland until 1 September.

entries.title: From The Odyssey’s Greece to House of the Dragon’s Spain, here’s where set-jetters may head in 2026
entries.description: The newly released Screen Tourism Forecast Index is predicting which countries and cities will see a surge in visitors thanks to film and TV this year.

entries.title: Pope Leo XIV to visit European migrant hotspot of Lampedusa on 4 July, Vatican says
entries.description: Under Pope Leo XIV, the Catholic Church has continued to call for their humane treatment around the world, including decrying mass deportations in his home country, the United States.

entries.title: Chancellor Merz sparks confusion with X post on World Cup exit
entries.description: "At this World Cup you thrilled our country," writes Chancellor Merz after the team’s exit, but reactions suggest he has badly misjudged the public mood.

entries.title: Chancellor Merz: X post on World Cup exit sparks confusion, then comes second post
entries.description: "You thrilled our country at this World Cup," writes Chancellor Merz after the team’s exit, but reactions show the public feels differently. A new post has since appeared on X.

entries.title: Chancellor Merz: why his confusing X posts on Germany's World Cup exit
entries.description: "You thrilled our country at this World Cup," wrote Chancellor Merz after the team’s exit – but reactions show he misread the mood. A second X post and his explanation for the debacle followed.

entries.title: Clashes erupt in the Netherlands after Morocco’s World Cup victory
entries.description: Celebrations for Morocco’s World Cup win over the Netherlands in The Hague turn into clashes with arrests made amid street unrest and festivities.

entries.title: Switzerland returns 18 looted Benin Bronzes to Nigeria
entries.description: Three Swiss museums have returned 18 Benin Bronzes to Nigeria, a new step in the African's country decades-long struggle to repatriate its looted cultural heritage.

entries.title: Morocco's World Cup win sparks celebrations and clashes in the Hague
entries.description: Morocco's victory sparked celebrations from Casablanca to the Netherlands, where jubilant scenes in one of Europe's largest Moroccan communities were later overshadowed by clashes with police.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 30th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 30th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Over 1 million migrants apply for Spain's mass regularisation, PM Pedro Sánchez says
entries.description: Sánchez has long argued that immigrants are needed to sustain the economy, the welfare state and pensions amid Spain's ageing population and depopulation in rural regions.

entries.title: Mamula Island by Banyan Tree opens in Montenegro, the luxury brand’s first resort in Europe
entries.description: Europe’s first Banyan Tree resort has opened on a secluded islet on the Bay of Kotor, revamping a centuries-old fortress into a luxury Adriatic escape.

entries.title: EU allocates steel import quotas to trading partners to curb import surge
entries.description: The new quotas will come into force on 1 July, with some preferential treatment agreed for partners bound by a trade agreement with the EU.

entries.title: US envoys Witkoff and Kushner land in Qatar for fresh Iran war talks
entries.description: Qatar said Trump's envoys arrived on Tuesday to meet mediators on "all regional issues," as Tehran confirmed sending its own delegation to Doha this week but denied agreeing to meet US counterparts "at any level". The visit follows four days of strikes in the Strait of Hormuz that paused on Monday.

entries.title: UK to spend 'record' €348 billion on defence over next four years, PM Starmer says
entries.description: The announcement follows months of wrangling within the government over the resources required to modernise the UK's armed forces in the face of rising threats, including from Russia.

entries.title: IKEA’s Dorothée Gardon on AI, the viral Punch moment and brand control in the social media age
entries.description: At Cannes Lions, IKEA’s Global Marketing Strategy & Integrated Media Director Dorothée Gardon spoke to Euronews Culture about how the Swedish brand is navigating a fast-changing media landscape shaped by AI and creativity.

entries.title: Lithuania's parliament approves Sinkevičius as prime minister
entries.description: Lithuania's parliament, the Seimas, on Tuesday approved Mindaugas Sinkevičius as the country's new prime minister — a third Social Democrat prime minister in this parliamentary term.

entries.title: Montenegro's ruling Europe Now! links up with EPP ahead of EU membership
entries.description: Montenegro's latest political milestone comes as the country enters what leaders describe as the "final lap" of its long campaign to become the European Union's next member state.

entries.title: At least four trapped after apartment block collapses in Athens, fire brigade says
entries.description: Emergency services have been deployed to the scene to help with the search and rescue operation and footage shown on local media showed workers walking in the rubble looking for signs of life.

entries.title: Von der Leyen visits Azerbaijan and Armenia to deepen South Caucasus ties
entries.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen begins the first visit to the South Caucasus since Armenia and Azerbaijan signed their historic peace agreement, now offering the EU the opportunities to build on the EU's strategic engagement with the region formerly in Russia's orbit.

entries.title: America can switch off the world's AI. Europe must switch gears before it's too late
entries.description: 'Europe is becoming a digital colony between two AI empires' writes Dr. Sergey Lagodinsky, Vice Chair of the Greens/EFA Group in the European Parlament in an OpEd for Euronews. For Brussels, it is time to act. Europe needs a smart strategy of cooperation that will keep the European economy alive.

entries.title: Thousands soaked in wine at Spain's iconic festival
entries.description: Thousands gather in Haro, Spain, for the La Rioja wine battle, spraying wine, dancing and climbing the hill to the church in a colourful festival.

entries.title: AI helps scientists decipher papyrus scroll burnt in Vesuvius eruption
entries.description: The scroll was first discovered in the 1750s but was too fragile to open. Using AI, scholars deciphered the full text and discovered a philosophical treaties on ethics and human progress.

entries.title: UK watchdog moves to break Apple and Google's grip on app payments
entries.description: Buy an app subscription today and there is a good chance Apple or Google is taking a cut. The CMA wants developers to be free to point customers elsewhere to pay and is asking the tech giants to justify any fees they charge for the privilege.

entries.title: Brytyjski regulator chce ograniczyć kontrolę Apple i Google nad płatnościami w aplikacjach
entries.description: Kupując dziś subskrypcję aplikacji, często część pieniędzy trafia do Apple lub Google. CMA chce, by twórcy mogli kierować klientów do innych metod płatności i domaga się od gigantów uzasadnienia pobieranych opłat.
